title: Lesson - How to Respond to a Customer

sections.content:
Open a case in the console to get started. In the case feed publishing section, click Email . You can insert merge fields, look up contacts, preview emails, clear emails and more from the email publication. You can also insert Salesforce Classic and Lightning Experience email templates, attach files, and remove attachments if you change your mind.


Here are a few things to keep in mind when working with email in Lightning Experience.

Look up contacts
To look up contact email addresses, click the lookup icon in the To, Cc or Bcc field. In the e-mail design, you can filter contacts by account contacts and by colleagues (internal users). To add contacts as email recipients, select the contacts and click Add . Don't see what you're looking for? You can search by name.

Attachments
To add more files from your computer, drag the files to the text area of ​​the email message. You can drag as many files as you want, but the files cannot be more than 2 GB. To attach files uploaded in Salesforce, use the attachment icon.

Braid field
Merge fields are only resolved when sending and previewing. The field appears as plain text when inserted into the publication.

TIP: You can insert merge fields that are not listed in the dialog box, such as merge fields for accounts and contacts. Enter them as plain text and use the preview button to review them before sending.

Email templates
You can embed Lightning Experience and Salesforce Classic templates. When you insert a template, the topic is replaced with the template's topic.
If you insert a responsive email that contains the HTML code <head>, <meta>, <title>or <style>, the template overwrites the content of the email body. But don't worry - we'll show you a confirmation message so you know before anything is overwritten.

Email draft
A working draft of email messages is saved every few seconds when you write, reply to, or forward an email message. You can leave it at any time and come back to it again later.

NOTE: Email drafts are private and cannot be shared. You can save only one draft per case. If you are working on a case with an auto-saved draft, you cannot write another email message until this draft has been sent or discarded. Your Salesforce admin must enable email drafts.

Email status
The email history displays responses sent by the Salesforce user, starting with the most recent response. The message details include an email status.
The status of an email message is Black when it has been replied to directly. For example, if you send an outgoing reply to an incoming email, the status will be Black. If you then send a subsequent outgoing reply to the same incoming email message, but do not reply directly to the first outgoing reply, the status of the first outgoing reply is set to Sent.

Email preview
Use Preview to review an email message and its merge fields before sending it.

Delete and empty email messages
The trash can icon deletes the working draft and resets the fields to their original state. The behavior differs depending on whether email drafts are enabled.
  • When email drafting is not enabled, this action clears the content, subject, and recipients that have been added and returns the email to its original state.
  • When email draft is enabled, it deletes the draft and then reverts it.

Opening of email design for anchoring
Open the docked email layout, which appears on the side of the console, making it easier to view the case feed while replying to an email. You can also maximize your email layout from the docked layout to help you focus on writing.
Click the open docked view icon on the email layout toolbar to open the docked layout.
If your administrator has enabled email drafts, the open to docked view button is disabled.

Embedded email editor
When you send an email with the anchored email editor, only a partial page refresh occurs.

Pictures
Embed images into messages by copying and pasting, providing image URLs, and browsing for or uploading Salesforce files. The way each method works is slightly different.
  • Copy and paste - supports all image file types. The image is added as an attachment and embedded, and the embedded image can be resized. Images in case emails cannot be larger than 1 MB. In other e-mail messages, the maximum is 5 MB. Microsoft Edge is not supported. Internet Explorer 11 or earlier does not allow image resizing.
  • Web URL: No maximum file size. The image is added inline and can be resized. The image is not added as an attachment, and it is not converted to a Salesforce file. The default description text for an image is set to User uploaded image.
  • Browse or Upload Salesforce File: Creates a Salesforce file from an uploaded file, or inserts an existing Salesforce file. The file size limit is based on Salesforce file limits. The image size cannot be changed from the message itself. The image is added as an attachment and embedded. If a content version for a Salesforce file changes, messages that use an older version of the Salesforce file are not updated.

NOTE: When using case email publishing in Lightning Experience, email attachments are included only when you forward messages, but not when you reply to messages.

Tables
To add a table to an email message, click the table icon in the email design's action menu and fill in the settings for the table. Then add the contents of the table. Use the action menu in the email design to apply a style to the content or insert images into the table. To edit the table's cell spacing, padding, and alignment, double-click a table border.
NOTE:
  • The table action is not available in the email template editor in Lightning.
  • Tables can be copied and pasted into the email design from other programs, but the style may be affected.
  • Depending on where you display a table in Salesforce, table borders can appear as a double or single line.

Code examples
Insert a code snippet that retains the code formatting by clicking the code example icon and pasting the code into the code window.
EMAIL STATUSDESCRIPTION
NewAn incoming email message that has not yet been read.
ReadAn incoming email message that has been read but not replied to.
SentAn outgoing email message.
BlackAny email that has been replied to. Reply to a sent e-mail message provides reply status.
